_comment: Source items the pipeline draws from. Each item produces exactly one carousel. The schema is intentionally minimal so it can carry research findings, product specs, recipes, statistics, anything structured.

items.raw_content: VERBATIM source text as it came from the original document. Never edited after seed. This is the audit trail.
items.sanitized_content: Sanitized version. Attributed, past tense, brand-safe. Claude only ever sees this version.
items.notes: Optional free-text field. Useful for context that doesn't belong in any other field.

items.raw_content: Another raw source, e.g. 'Recent industry survey shows 64% of customers prefer X over Y'.
items.sanitized_content: A 2025 industry survey of [N] respondents reported 64% preference for X over Y, with margin of error +/- 3%.

items.raw_content: NVIDIA Q4 FY2026 revenue hit $39.3B, up 69% YoY. Data Center segment alone was $35.6B, up 73% YoY. Gaming revenue was $2.5B. Q1 FY2027 guidance: $43B +/- 2%. CEO Jensen Huang: demand for Blackwell GPU infrastructure remains 'exceptional'. EPS (non-GAAP) $0.89 vs analyst consensus $0.85.
items.sanitized_content: NVIDIA reported Q4 FY2026 revenue of $39.3 billion, a 69% year-over-year increase, per the company's February 2026 earnings release. The Data Center segment reported $35.6 billion in revenue, 73% year-over-year growth. Gaming segment revenue was $2.5 billion. The company provided Q1 FY2027 revenue guidance of approximately $43 billion. Non-GAAP EPS of $0.89 exceeded FactSet consensus estimates of $0.85.
items.attribution: NVIDIA Corporation Q4 FY2026 Earnings Release, February 2026. FactSet consensus data.
items.notes: Power demo item for financial analyst voice. Pair with config/brand/voice-analyst.md. Tripwire category 'financial_guarantees' is active — avoid any forward-looking phrasing beyond what the company itself stated in the earnings release.

_status_values_comment: status enum: 'unused' | 'drafted' | 'approved' | 'published' | 'retired'. The pipeline updates this field automatically as items move through the stages. 'retired' is set manually when a source is invalidated and should not be reused.
_id_comment: IDs are stable forever. Once an item exists with id 'item-007', that ID is never reused — even if the item is retired.
_template_comment: Must reference a file in config/templates/. The default template is 'default'. Add 'comparison', 'narrative', etc. as needed by creating new template files.
